children.text: AXIAL FIELD STUDIES: A FICTIONAL MONOGRAPH ON PROVINCIAL MOBILIZATION
children.label: title

children.text: Argument Overview
children.label: section_header

children.text: The Halberstadt convergence thesis holds that provincial guilds mobilize not when grievances peak but when rival factions within the guild converge on a single shared claim, a convergence that manufactures collective capacity out of previously fragmented discontent.
children.label: text

children.text: This convergence dynamic, rather than any single triggering grievance, best explains why petitioning waves in the provincial guild records cluster tightly in time even where the underlying grievances themselves long predate the wave.
children.label: text

children.text: Conclusion
children.label: section_header
